allies.rationale_text: Empathy strengthens feedback-models-sbi by helping the giver model how the receiver will interpret the situation, impact, and invitation.
allies.source_quote: To provide meaningful feedback, the giver must adopt the receiver's Point of View.

allies.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory strengthens feedback-models-sbi by breaking corrective information into manageable chunks the receiver can process.
allies.source_quote: Structured feedback like scaffolding is a powerful ally because it breaks complex problems into bite-sized chunks.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Feedback-models-sbi conflicts with confirmation-bias when the receiver dismisses disconfirming evidence that the structured conversation is trying to surface.
structured_tensions.source_quote: Structured feedback inherently conflicts with confirmation bias because it often introduces disconfirming evidence.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Feedback-models-sbi conflicts with abstraction when high-level judgments crowd out the concrete behavioral bricks the receiver can actually work with.
structured_tensions.source_quote: The structured, data-driven conclusions of experts are often fiercely defended, making them antagonists to the simple, concrete bricks required for effective feedback.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved only current-vocabulary first-order seams that were explicit or cleanly warranted by the raw source.
donor_drops: Did not keep donor-shaped or out-of-vocabulary relation surfaces; omission beat weak forcing.
open_questions: Higher-order candidates remain observational only and belong in later selective review rather than in this closure batch.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only for this model.
antagonists: Any broader antagonistic surface not preserved here was omitted because the current first-order vocabulary did not justify forcing it.
structured_tensions: Only explicit or cleanly source-supported tensions that survive current-vocabulary discipline were preserved.
